hints: If total gas is less than total cost, no solution exists — otherwise a greedy single pass finds the start.
Track a running tank total; whenever negative, the current start can't work, reset to the next station.
greedy.description: Gas Station: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Activity selection.

greedy.name: Partition Labels
hints: Each character can only appear within one partition — a partition must extend to the last occurrence of every character in it.
Precompute the last index of every character; extend the boundary to the max last-occurrence seen so far.
greedy.description: Partition Labels: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Activity selection.

greedy.name: Candy
hints: Left-to-right enforces increasing slopes from the left.
Right-to-left fixes the other direction while keeping max of both constraints.
greedy.triggerQuestion: What phrase in the prompt reveals the intended pattern?
greedy.pitfall: Write the brute force first, then name the repeated work or invariant before coding.
greedy.description: Candy: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Two-pass Greedy.

greedy.name: Patching Array
hints: Maintain the smallest missing sum miss that current numbers can cover up to miss-1.
If next num <= miss, extend coverage; otherwise patch with miss.
greedy.triggerQuestion: What constraint makes the usual brute force too slow?
greedy.pitfall: The invariant is coverage range, not local numeric closeness.
greedy.description: Patching Array: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Greedy Coverage Range.

trie-bits.name: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
hints: Each node holds a map/array of children (one per character) plus a flag for 'end of word'.
Insert/search walk down one character at a time, creating nodes as needed for insert, failing early for search.
trie-bits.description: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree): traverse tree structure with correct state propagation and base cases. Pattern focus: Trie.

trie-bits.name: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
hints: Standard trie insert, but search needs to support a wildcard '.' matching any character.
When you hit a '.', try all possible children at that position recursively instead of just one.
trie-bits.description: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Trie.

trie-bits.name: Word Search II
hints: Combine Word Search's grid DFS with a Trie built from all target words, to prune paths no word starts with.
As you DFS the grid, walk the Trie in parallel — if the path isn't a valid Trie prefix, stop exploring.
trie-bits.description: Word Search II: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Trie.

trie-bits.name: Single Number
hints: XOR-ing a number with itself gives 0, XOR-ing with 0 gives the number back — what happens if you XOR the whole array?
XOR is commutative and associative, so pairs cancel out, leaving only the single unpaired number.
trie-bits.description: Single Number: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Bit Manipulation / XOR.

trie-bits.name: Number of 1 Bits
hints: Repeatedly clearing the lowest set bit (n & (n-1)) removes one '1' bit at a time.
Alternatively, shift and check the lowest bit (n & 1) in a loop of 32 iterations.
trie-bits.description: Number of 1 Bits: compute the total number of valid outcomes under the given constraints. Pattern focus: Bit Manipulation / XOR.

trie-bits.name: Counting Bits
hints: The bit count of n relates to the bit count of a smaller already-computed number — think about n >> 1 or n & (n-1).
dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1) builds the whole answer array from previously computed smaller values.
trie-bits.description: Counting Bits: compute the total number of valid outcomes under the given constraints. Pattern focus: Bit Manipulation / XOR.

trie-bits.name: Missing Number
hints: XOR-ing all array values with all indices from 0 to n cancels out every present number, leaving the missing one.
Alternatively, use the arithmetic sum formula for 0..n and subtract the actual array sum.
trie-bits.description: Missing Number: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Bit Manipulation / XOR.

trie-bits.name: Sum of Two Integers
hints: Without + or -, XOR gives sum-without-carry, and AND-shifted-left gives the carry.
Repeat: sum = a XOR b, carry = (a AND b) << 1, then a = sum, b = carry, until carry becomes 0.
trie-bits.description: Sum of Two Integers: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Bit Manipulation / XOR.

trie-bits.name: Replace Words
hints: Insert all dictionary roots into a trie, then scan each word until you hit the shortest matching root.
The answer for each word is the first prefix that exists as a complete trie word.
trie-bits.description: Replace Words: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Trie.

trie-bits.name: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
hints: For each bit, greedily prefer the opposite bit already in the trie.
Build the answer from high bits to low bits.
trie-bits.triggerQuestion: What phrase in the prompt reveals the intended pattern?
trie-bits.pitfall: Write the brute force first, then name the repeated work or invariant before coding.
trie-bits.description: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array: optimize for the best possible value while preserving problem constraints. Pattern focus: Bit Trie / Greedy Bits.

trie-bits.name: Single Number II
hints: Count each bit modulo 3, or use ones/twos state masks.
Negative numbers work when you reconstruct bits carefully.
trie-bits.triggerQuestion: What constraint makes the usual brute force too slow?
trie-bits.pitfall: XOR alone only works for pairs, not triples.
trie-bits.description: Single Number II: identify constraints, choose an efficient strategy, and handle edge cases. Pattern focus: Bit Counting / State Machine.

revision.name: Contains Duplicate
hints: A set can tell you in O(1) whether you've seen a value before.
Walk the array once, adding to a set — if you ever see a value already there, you're done.
revision.description: Return whether any value appears at least twice in the array.

revision.name: Subarray Sum Equals K
hints: Prefix sums plus a hashmap of prefix-sum frequencies is the key combo here.
If prefixSum[j] - prefixSum[i] = k, a subarray sums to k — track how often each prefix sum occurs.
revision.description: Count contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k by tracking how often each prefix sum has appeared.

revision.name: Fibonacci Number
hints: Think about what smaller subproblem this reduces to — fib(n) depends on fib(n-1) and fib(n-2).
Once plain recursion works, notice you're recomputing the same values — that's your cue to memoize or go bottom-up.
revision.description: Return the nth Fibonacci value by defining the recurrence clearly and avoiding repeated recomputation.

problemIntentRules.intent: identify the complement, ordering, or invariant that lets you build valid combinations without brute force
track character frequency or mapping constraints so each symbol relationship stays valid
transform repeated range computation into running-prefix relationships you can query in O(1)
maintain a valid contiguous window while updating only what changes at the boundaries
expand or compare symmetrically from the center while preserving palindrome conditions
use monotonic structure to cut the search space in half after defining the true/false boundary
move pointers carefully while preserving node connections and avoiding lost references
store unresolved state in LIFO order so the next qualifying element can resolve it efficiently
build candidates incrementally, prune invalid branches early, and backtrack cleanly
define what each recursive call returns and how subtree results combine at the current node
keep only the most important elements in heap order so each update stays efficient
propagate the best-known arrival state through neighbors and only continue from states that improve the answer
treat the board as a graph and traverse each reachable region exactly once
model dependency edges and process only nodes whose prerequisites are already satisfied
merge connected components and detect when two nodes already share the same parent set
write the recurrence for take-vs-skip decisions and carry only the state needed for the next position
define DP states around capacity or target value and transition by taking or skipping the current choice
track the best subsequence state so each new element extends previously solved structure
sort by the governing boundary first, then sweep while merging or counting overlaps
index shared prefixes so lookup work is proportional to characters rather than total words
reason in binary and use bitwise invariants to cancel noise or isolate the needed value
